oop_exam_questions.question: Object-Oriented Programming is primarily based on:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: OOP organizes programs around objects created from classes.

oop_exam_questions.question: A class is:
options: A. An instance of an object
B. A blueprint for creating objects
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: B. A blueprint for creating objects
oop_exam_questions.explanation: A class defines properties and behaviors for objects.

oop_exam_questions.question: An object is:
options: B. An instance of a class
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: B. An instance of a class
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Objects are created from classes and have actual state and behavior.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which of the following best represents the state of an object?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: The state of an object is represented by its data values.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which of the following best represents the behavior of an object?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Methods define what an object can do.

oop_exam_questions.question: Encapsulation means:
options: A. Hiding data and bundling it with methods
B. Reusing a parent class only
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: A. Hiding data and bundling it with methods
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Encapsulation binds data and methods together and restricts direct access.

oop_exam_questions.question: Data hiding is achieved in OOP mainly through:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Private and protected members help hide internal implementation.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which access specifier makes members accessible only within the same class?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Private members are accessible only within the class.

oop_exam_questions.question: Abstraction means:
options: A. Showing only essential features and hiding details
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: A. Showing only essential features and hiding details
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Abstraction focuses on relevant behavior while hiding implementation details.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which of the following is the best example of abstraction?
options: A. Using a car without knowing engine internals
B. Writing all code in one function
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: A. Using a car without knowing engine internals
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Abstraction lets users work with simplified interfaces.

oop_exam_questions.question: Inheritance allows:
options: A. One class to acquire properties and behavior of another
B. Only objects to inherit from functions
C. Data to be deleted automatically
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: A. One class to acquire properties and behavior of another
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Inheritance promotes reuse by deriving new classes from existing ones.

oop_exam_questions.question: The existing class from which another class derives is called:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: The base class provides inherited members to derived classes.

oop_exam_questions.question: A class that inherits from another class is called:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: The derived class extends or specializes the base class.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which type of inheritance involves one base class and one derived class?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Single inheritance uses one parent and one child relationship.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which type of inheritance allows a class to inherit from more than one base class?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Multiple inheritance means one class derives from multiple base classes.

oop_exam_questions.question: Polymorphism means:
options: A. Many forms of the same interface or function
B. One class cannot have methods
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: A. Many forms of the same interface or function
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Polymorphism allows the same operation to behave differently in different contexts.

oop_exam_questions.question: Method overloading is an example of:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Overloading is resolved by the compiler based on method signature.

oop_exam_questions.question: Method overriding is an example of:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Overriding is typically resolved at runtime through dynamic dispatch.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which of the following distinguishes overloading from overriding?
options: A. Overloading uses same method name with different parameters
D. Overriding changes only variable names
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: A. Overloading uses same method name with different parameters
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Overloading differs by parameter list, while overriding redefines inherited methods.

oop_exam_questions.question: Overriding generally requires:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: A derived class overrides a method inherited from a base class.

oop_exam_questions.question: A constructor is:
options: A. A method used to destroy objects
B. A special method used to initialize objects
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: B. A special method used to initialize objects
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Constructors are called when an object is created.

oop_exam_questions.question: A default constructor is one that:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: A default constructor can be called without passing parameters.

oop_exam_questions.question: A destructor is used to:
options: C. Release resources when an object is destroyed
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: C. Release resources when an object is destroyed
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Destructors help clean up resources at object destruction.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which binding occurs at runtime?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Dynamic binding resolves method calls at runtime.

oop_exam_questions.question: Message passing in OOP means:
options: A. Objects communicate by invoking methods on one another
B. Messages are sent only over networks
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: A. Objects communicate by invoking methods on one another
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Objects interact through method calls, often described as message passing.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which access specifier usually gives the widest access?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Public members can generally be accessed from outside the class.

oop_exam_questions.question: Protected members are generally accessible in:
options: A. The class and its derived classes
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: A. The class and its derived classes
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Protected members are visible within the class and subclasses.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which of the following best supports code reusability?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Inheritance enables reuse and extension of existing class behavior.

oop_exam_questions.question: If a method has the same name in parent and child class, and child provides a new definition, it is:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: The child method replaces the inherited behavior through overriding.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which feature allows the same function name to work differently with different parameter lists?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Overloading provides same method name with different signatures.

oop_exam_questions.question: A real-world example of inheritance is:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Car can inherit common properties from Vehicle.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which feature improves security by restricting direct data access?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Encapsulation helps protect data from improper access.

oop_exam_questions.question: An abstract class is mainly used to:
options: A. Instantiate many objects directly only
B. Provide a common base with partial implementation
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: B. Provide a common base with partial implementation
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Abstract classes define common structure and behavior for derived classes.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which principle says one interface, many implementations?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Polymorphism allows a common interface to represent multiple behaviors.

oop_exam_questions.question: If all members of a class are public, then data hiding is:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Public access exposes internal data and weakens encapsulation.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which of the following is not an OOP feature?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Packet switching is a networking concept, not an OOP concept.

oop_exam_questions.question: An object occupies:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Objects are runtime entities and consume memory.

oop_exam_questions.question: Creating an object from a class is called:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Instantiation is the process of creating an object from a class.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which relationship is stronger in OOP?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Inheritance is commonly described as an 'is-a' relationship.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which relationship best describes composition?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Composition indicates one object contains or uses another.

oop_exam_questions.question: The main benefit of abstraction is:
options: B. Reduced complexity for the user
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: B. Reduced complexity for the user
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Abstraction helps users interact with simpler views of complex systems.

oop_exam_questions.question: Dynamic binding is closely associated with:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Runtime method selection is typically used with overriding.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit of OOP?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: OOP supports modular design, reuse, and maintainability.

oop_exam_questions.question: A constructor usually has:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Constructors do not specify a return type.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which of these best represents compile-time polymorphism?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Compile-time polymorphism is resolved by the compiler.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which of the following is true for objects of the same class?
options: A. They always have identical data values
B. They share common structure and behavior definition
C. They are stored in ROM only
oop_exam_questions.correct_answer: B. They share common structure and behavior definition
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Objects of the same class follow the same class blueprint.

oop_exam_questions.question: When one class uses another class as a member, it is often called:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Composition is a has-a relationship between classes.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which keyword idea is most associated with hiding implementation and exposing interface?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Abstraction presents the essential interface while hiding details.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which of the following usually improves maintainability in OOP?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Well-encapsulated modules are easier to maintain.

oop_exam_questions.question: Polymorphism with parent reference referring to child object is commonly used to achieve:
oop_exam_questions.explanation: This enables runtime selection of the appropriate overridden method.

oop_exam_questions.question: Which feature prevents unauthorized direct access to internal object data?
oop_exam_questions.explanation: Encapsulation controls access to object internals.
